scenario_results.reason: The lane validated seasonal context and artifact set, produced handoff summary with prior owner, destination owner, and blockers intact.
Missing supplier authorization, environmental constraints, and destination acceptance were surfaced as blockers and required follow-up.
Absent destination owner and traceability identifiers blocked handoff execution and returned required-acceptance prerequisites.
Conflicting ownership and schedule claims were captured as a dependency overlap and returned as blocker routing rather than hidden.
Traceability conflict across systems preserved as evidence mismatch and escalation-required condition.
Environmental and food-safety holds were treated as blocking conditions requiring specialist escalation and no in-lane release.
The request was correctly routed away from agronomic/veterinary signoff, with explicit boundaries and next owner.
Execution ownership was refused and rerouted to adjacent teams with required blocker and artifact checklist.
Missing tenant policy and grant-context facts triggered low-confidence escalation instead of guessed routing.
Seasonal dependency gap correctly blocked movement despite mixed commodity readiness until season constraints and policy overrides were supplied.
Conflicting public-versus-grant policy was escalated and routed for human resolution with uncertainty flagged.
Closure was withheld despite resolved shortage because permit/evidence and destination proof remained incomplete.

notes: All 12 scenarios pass against the standard 0.90 threshold and cover coordination, handoff, seasonal, boundary, and escalation behavior in the agricultural supply chain context.
Functionality coverage includes shared abilities, all coordination-handoffs-and-routing abilities, and industry-profile abilities for agriculture-and-natural-resources.
